report.decision_reason: BLOCKED — 3 critical P1 security findings (SQL injection CWE-89, hardcoded credentials CWE-798, command injection CWE-78) represent immediate exploitation vectors. Score of 0/100 is well below the 50-point threshold for blocking. All three P1 issues require remediation before any further review.

top_issues: SQL Injection via f-string in execute()
Hardcoded database password in source code
Multiple imports on one line (PEP 8 violation)

critical_findings.title: SQL Injection via f-string interpolation
critical_findings.code_snippet: query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '{username}'"
critical_findings.description: User-controlled input is interpolated directly into a SQL query string via f-string, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands. Both static analysis (P1 S364) and security scan (CRITICAL CWE-89) flagged this independently.
critical_findings.impact: Remote data exfiltration, unauthorized data access, data destruction, authentication bypass
critical_findings.recommendation: Replace f-string interpolation with parameterized queries: `conn.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?', (username,))`

critical_findings.title: Hardcoded Database Password
critical_findings.description: A database password literal 'SuperSecret123!' is hardcoded in source code. Both static analysis (P1 S105) and security scan (CRITICAL CWE-798) flagged this. Note: DB_PASSWORD is dead code (unused), but the credential exposure is separate from the dead code concern.
critical_findings.impact: Credential leakage via version control, insider exposure, failed compliance audits (PCI-DSS, SOC2, ISO 27001)
critical_findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credential. Use environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., `os.environ.get('DB_PASSWORD')` or HashiCorp Vault).

critical_findings.title: OS Command Injection via shell=True
critical_findings.description: Shell=True combined with f-string interpolation allows an attacker to inject arbitrary OS commands through the `cmd` parameter. Static analysis flagged P1 S605; security scan flagged HIGH CWE-78.
critical_findings.impact: Full remote command execution on the host system
critical_findings.recommendation: Replace with list-based arguments and remove shell=True: `subprocess.call(['echo', cmd])` or use `shlex.quote()` if shell is unavoidable.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Deserialization via pickle.load()
high_findings.code_snippet: with open(filename, 'rb') as f: return pickle.load(f)
high_findings.description: pickle.load() deserializes arbitrary objects from untrusted files, enabling remote code execution. Static analysis flagged this as P3 B301 (appeared twice — deduplicated); security scan flagged as HIGH CWE-502.
high_findings.impact: Remote code execution if an attacker controls the input file (RCE vector via __reduce__)
high_findings.recommendation: Replace pickle with a safe serialization format (JSON with schema validation). If pickle is unavoidable, authenticate and validate the source before deserialization.

medium_findings.title: Use of Unsafe Module: import pickle
medium_findings.description: The `pickle` module is imported, which is inherently unsafe for deserialization of untrusted data. Static analysis flagged W0611 (unused import — may be a false positive since pickle is used on line 8); security scan flagged MEDIUM CWE-1104.
medium_findings.impact: pickle enables arbitrary code execution during deserialization; its presence in imports signals potential RCE vector
medium_findings.recommendation: Remove pickle import and replace with `json` or another safe serialization format

medium_findings.title: Use of Subprocess Module (Command Injection Risk Vector)
medium_findings.description: The `subprocess` module is imported and used with shell=True on line 10, creating a command injection risk. Security scan flagged MEDIUM CWE-1104.
medium_findings.impact: Facilitates OS command injection when combined with shell=True and untrusted input
medium_findings.recommendation: Restrict subprocess usage to list-based calls without shell=True, or replace with safer alternatives (e.g., shutil)

medium_findings.title: Missing Zero-Division Guard
medium_findings.code_snippet: def divide(a, b): return a / b
medium_findings.description: The divide() function performs division without checking if the divisor is zero, which will raise a ZeroDivisionError at runtime. Static analysis flagged P3 W1201.
medium_findings.impact: Runtime exception / unhandled crash when b=0
medium_findings.recommendation: Add guard: `if b == 0: raise ValueError('Division by zero')` or return appropriate default

low_findings.title: Multiple Imports on One Line
low_findings.description: Multiple modules are imported on a single line, violating PEP 8 (E401). Static analysis flagged P4 E401.
low_findings.recommendation: Split into separate imports per line per PEP 8

low_findings.title: Path Traversal — No Input Validation in load_data()
low_findings.code_snippet:
def load_data(filename):
    with open(filename, 'rb') as f: return pickle.load(f)
low_findings.description: The filename parameter in load_data() is not validated or sanitized, allowing potential path traversal attacks (e.g., '../../etc/passwd'). Security scan flagged LOW CWE-22.
low_findings.impact: Unauthorized file reads outside intended directory
low_findings.recommendation: Validate filename against an allowed set or use `os.path.basename()` and restrict to a designated data directory

info_findings.title: Dead Code — DB_PASSWORD Assigned but Never Read
info_findings.description: DB_PASSWORD is assigned but never referenced anywhere in the codebase. Static analysis flagged P5 R0201. This is doubly problematic: it's both dead code AND a hardcoded secret.
info_findings.impact: Unnecessary code clutter; the hardcoded credential is the more serious concern (see FIND-002)
info_findings.recommendation: Remove the dead code. If the password is needed, use it properly and source from environment variables.

recommendations.action: Convert f-string query to parameterized query with `?` placeholders
Remove password literal, use environment variable or secrets manager
Replace shell=True with list arguments and remove f-string interpolation
Replace pickle.load() with JSON deserialization or authenticate input source
Add validation for zero divisor in divide()
Consider replacing pickle and restricting subprocess usage patterns
Split imports, validate file paths, remove dead code

score_breakdown.notes: Score floored at 0. The P1 cap (-80 max) prevented -90, but total deductions still exceeded 100.
